stages.result: auditor table matches lead observations exactly: plan-check 0, xform PASS/0, wire PASS_UNPROVEN/1, acceptance 0, ship 0 + porcelain empty, status 0 (2 built), handoff:check 0

outcomeNote: FAIL per the letter of Stage 5 (uf-fit-wire cannot exceed PASS_UNPROVEN under --strict after two fresh design attempts — tooling limitation, frictions[4]). The feature itself is fully built: status 2/2 BUILT, acceptance 4/4 green, uf-fit-xform strict PASS, ship green, scope clean, zero violations.
assumptions: Stage 2 human approval assumed per Chris's run instruction; lead recorded plan.verdicts accept x2 and re-ran approve-export after the plan amendment under the same assumption.
Stage 8 humanCheck deferred to Chris's next run (zoom into a node, toggle the parent group — camera must not move).
frictions: Stage order defect for add-changes: verify-change's structural check only sees nodes already in the map, so writeback+bundle (Stage 6) must precede Stage 5 for adds; ran writeback mid-Stage-5 to unblock.

novakai:writeback --add-from-plan writes the plan's namespaced ref verbatim into the fragment and never emits %% src (M9's known shim precedent) — needed a manual fragment fix + %% src append, one extra spawn.
plan-check and cert both accept a modify change whose fm lacks interfaces — the gap only surfaces at Stage 5 as structural 'drifted'.
Once writeback lands an added node, plan-check REAL-IDS rejects status 'add' (node now exists); flipped uf-fit-xform to 'modify' to stay truthful — the plan schema lacks a landed-add state.

BLOCKING (forces outcome FAIL): the acceptance runner resolves a change's cases via the map's %% src before the acceptance block's own path/symbol, and unfold.ts is unimportable by the runner (wasm import) — a modify target that is a non-exported closure can never be behaviourally proven, so uf-fit-wire is capped at PASS_UNPROVEN under --strict. Fix requires tools/: honor acc.path/symbol, or allow proof-by-dep.
